message:
fix(doctor): snapshot and restore tombstones across --repair's JSONL rebuild

`doctor --repair` falls through to `config::repair_database_from_jsonl`
whenever the light repair passes (blocked-cache rebuild, sidecar
quarantine, REINDEX, VACUUM, post-repair write probe) can't clear the
report. That JSONL rebuild only imports what the on-disk JSONL holds,
so any tombstone the user had deleted but not yet flushed was silently
wiped — the deletion-retention state lived only in the DB, not the
JSONL, so the rebuild reimported the issue as open.

This is the same hazard that `br sync --rebuild` already guards (via
snapshot_tombstones + restore_tombstones around its reset/import
cycle, in both the in-place and delegated auto-recovery paths) and
that startup auto-recovery just learned to guard in a0d95ee.
`doctor --repair`'s own fallback was the last rebuild surface without
preservation.

Fix: add a best-effort `preserved_tombstones_for_doctor_rebuild`
helper that tries to open the pre-repair DB, snapshots its tombstones,
filters out any already present in the JSONL as tombstones (the
rebuild will bring those back on its own), and returns the remainder.
Every step degrades safely — DB file missing, DB unopenable, JSONL
unreadable, per-tombstone relation failures — so the rebuild never
fails because preservation failed. After the rebuild, we
`restore_tombstones` atomically inside a write transaction and mark
them dirty so the next flush re-exports them.

Also rustfmt cleanups the move surfaced: the `cli/commands/sync.rs`
test-module `use` block now sorts `crate::sync::{…}` with the rest of
the `crate::*` imports, and the config-side preservation helper call
collapses onto one line per rustfmt.

Regression test: e2e_doctor_repair_preserves_unflushed_tombstones
creates two issues, flushes so the JSONL shows both open, deletes one
with --no-auto-flush (tombstone only in DB), injects duplicate
config.issue_prefix rows so the next open reports an error, runs
`doctor --repair --json`, then asserts `br show <deleted-id> --json`
still returns status=tombstone. Without the fix, the repair's JSONL
rebuild would reimport the issue as open and the assertion would fail
with status="open".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
